programme.sub-title: Judge Halts Court So Brooks Can Rant

programme.title: Deadly Parade Crash Trial
programme.desc: Jurors hear the timeline of events; Brooks drove his mother's SUV into the crowd, then abandoned it.

programme.title: Deadly Parade Crash Trial
programme.desc: The cross-examination wasn't over yet on the man who saw the abandoned SUV parked near his home, after the deadly parade crash. He said it was damaged when he saw it.

programme.title: Deadly Parade Crash Trial
programme.desc: The owner of the house where Darrell Brooks ditched his SUV testifies.

programme.title: Deadly Parade Crash Trial
programme.sub-title: Barefooted, Running, And Asking For A Ride
programme.desc: The State called people who lived near the deadly parade crash to identify Darrell Brooks as the one who abandoned an SUV and ran away. Police said it was that vehicle that rammed into the crowd killing six people and injuring more than 60 others.

programme.title: Deadly Parade Crash Trial
programme.sub-title: Mom Calls For A Lyft Ride
programme.desc: Darrell Brooks's mother called for a ride minutes after the deadly Christmas parade crash. Brooks was accused of ramming into the crowd and killing six people. Police said he abandoned the SUV and ran away in a residential area.

programme.title: Wrong Apartment Murder Trial
programme.desc: The Defense brought in expert testimony to show the reality of what police officers go through.

programme.title: Wrong Apartment Murder Trial
programme.desc: The shooting was classified as a police-involved shooting.

programme.title: Wrong Apartment Murder Trial
programme.desc: The state began its fiery closing argument dismissing the defense's claim of mistaken identity.

programme.title: Wrong Apartment Murder Trial
programme.desc: The jury heard intense closing arguments; the defense pleaded with jurors.

programme.title: Sex, Cyanide And A Doctor On The Run Case
programme.desc: Jamal Khalife testified that while living abroad the defendant drank a fifth of liquor and did drugs every day. This was on top of allegedly promising marriage to three different women in exchange for sex. Next, a police officer from the country of Cyprus took the stand. Yazeed Essa had been wanted in the death of his wife, Rosemarie, for 18 months. But then he flew from Lebanon to Cyprus. As soon as he got off the plane he was immediately arrested and held for potential extradition.

programme.title: Sex, Cyanide And A Doctor On The Run Case
programme.desc: Jurors were brought back to the defendant's medical school training and education.

programme.title: Sex, Cyanide And A Doctor On The Run Case
programme.desc: The ER doctor and good friend of Yazeed Essa was under cross-examination. The two learned about cyanide poisoning as medical school residents. Prosecutors wanted to link the defendant's knowledge of the poison to the death of his wife, Rosemarie. Next, jurors heard from the victim's OB-GYN. She confirmed the supplements she was taking at the time she died.

programme.title: Sex, Cyanide And A Doctor On The Run Case
programme.desc: Dr. Yazeed Essa is alleged to have poisoned his wife, Rosemarie Essa, to death with cyanide. Essa fled the country when he became a suspect, prompting an international manhunt. He was arrested in Cyprus and extradited back to the U.S. to stand trial.

programme.title: Interview With a Killer

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: In 1979, 16-year-old Kim Bryant disappears from outside a Dairy Queen in Las Vegas. Three weeks later, her body is found: she had been sexually assaulted and bludgeoned to death. 40 years later, genetic genealogy finally names the killer.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: A 65-year-old mystery is solved when DNA testing identifies the remains of 7-year-old Chester Alfred Breiney.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: When a beloved young woman is brutally murdered and left in an abandoned house in Brown County, Texas, investigative genetic genealogy could be the key to finding the perpetrator.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives
programme.desc: "Ice Cold Murder" is the story of the dedicated investigation into Christy Mirack's murder and how the Bloodline Detectives traced her killer with DNA.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: Huntington Beach, 1968. Boys on their way to school discover the brutalized body of a young woman in an oil field. Police are unable to identify her or her killer. It will take 52 years before DNA science is able to solve this double mystery.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: A young woman is found beaten, raped and strangled in Garden Grove, California in 1987. Two years pass until a woman in a neighboring jurisdiction meets the same fate. It will take years before forensics and police identify this serial killer.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: In July 1967, 20-year-old Susan Galvin is found dead in a parking garage elevator at Seattle Center. After over 50 years, detectives reopen the investigation with the help of forensic genetic genealogy in 2018.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: DNA and genealogy reveal the identity of a man responsible for violent home invasions from 1982 to 1997.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: Thirty years after college student Cathy Sposito is brutally killed on a hiking trail in Prescott, Arizona, can a groundbreaking forensic tool finally identify her killer?

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives
programme.desc: This episode follows how with the help of familial DNA detectives were able to track down a serial rapist.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace

programme.desc: In October 1993, a robber surprised unsuspecting customers by dropping through the roof of a California bank.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1984, the son of a successful real estate developer was kidnapped in Miami. After the father received a $3 million ransom demand from a self-proclaimed terrorist, the FBI became involved and agents began to suspect a pair of Colombian nationals.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1976, a car bombing in Washington DC's Embassy Row claimed the lives of a former Chilean ambassador.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: This special episode examines previous cases to learn about the investigative techniques used by the FBI.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: The Los Macheteros terrorist organization sought to end U.S. rule of Puerto Rico through violence. When the group claimed responsibility for an attack on 18 unarmed servicemen traveling near San Juan, the FBI is called in to investigate the killers.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: After a parole officer was found shot dead in his car, the FBI, DEA and NYPD struggled to contain the mounting violence from drug gangs that continued to escalate. Authorities sought to put two ruthless drug kingpins behind bars for the crime.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1988, a criminal confessed that two community members hired him to murder a South Carolina farmer.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In the early 1990s, a gang based in the U.S. Virgin Islands smuggled large amounts of cocaine into mainland America. Police and federal agents targeted the violent group, but its leader remained a mystery.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Amid the cocaine wars of 1980s Miami, a multi-agency tactical squad suspected that a group of cops might be involved in illegal drug busts. The squad uncovered a ring of police who were making millions by ripping off smuggled cocaine.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In the summer of 1998, a wealthy woman disappeared from her home in Jackson, Mississippi. The only clue was a mysterious ransom note listing twelve names but no specific instructions. The FBI struggled to find answers in the cryptic message.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1993, an elderly man was found suffocated in his home with his checkbook, credit cards and vehicle stolen.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In Richmond, Virginia, two masked men who were robbing a bank escaped after opening fire on officers responding to the call. Agents found one bank teller dead, another wounded; the bank's security guard had been shot four times yet was still alive.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Ali Qazimaj is an Albanian conman who brutally murdered an elderly couple in 2016.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Maria's marriage crumbled when Malcolm discovered her bigamy. As he moved on and found new love, Maria's jealousy erupted into dangerous rage, leading to a confrontation that exposed how far she was willing to go.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Stephen Farrow committed two murders in early 2012 motivated by an obsessive hatred of Christianity, which he attributed to sexual abuse from a priest.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The story of the I-95 killer, who brutally killed six men in 1994 before being executed three years later.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The chilling story of Mark Martin, who murdered three homeless women in his quest to become Nottingham's first serial killer.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Billy Mansfield Jr. moved between Florida and Michigan, targeting vulnerable women and girls. Between 1975 and 1980, Mansfield Jr. murdered at least five victims, burying four of them at his family home in Spring Hill, Florida.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: One of the UK's most infamous serial killers, Joanna Dennehy brutally stabbed three men to death.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: Edward Johnson is sent to prison for the brutal murder of a female friend, but the violence doesn't end there.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: One of the most feared men in US prison history, Thomas Silverstein became a gang leader for the Aryan Brotherhood on the inside. After savagely killing multiple inmates and a correctional officer, he spent 36 years in solitary confinement.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: Nothing was going to stop Ricky Dubose from breaking free of prison. Whilst being transported on a bus between facilities, he killed two correctional officers before escaping - and going on the run.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: A father took his children out for food after murdering his wife, then strangled them when they returned home.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: A dangerous sexual predator murders a young woman, just days after his release from prison.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Over six years, Eddie Seda, the copycat Zodiac killer stalked the streets of New York City, in search of easy targets. His cowardly attacks culminated with the ferocious murder of a woman, who had only asked him for a cigarette.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: A routine traffic stop revealed a body in Joel's car, unraveling a long string of murders targeting vulnerable women. The discovery ended his violent reign and gave investigators answers long out of reach.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: "Pee Wee" grew from a petty criminal into a violent predator. When a teen disappeared, detectives uncovered his escalating brutality, realizing he had far more blood on his hands than anyone suspected.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Victor Farrant is a convicted rapist who murdered his former girlfriend in 1996 and attempted to murder another woman after he was released from prison.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: In 2003, Kenneth Regan and William Horncy murdered a millionaire and his young family. The killers wiped out three generations of the Chohan family, including two young children.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: When Carol Jarvis disappeared, her husband told friends and family that she'd simply gone on a little holiday.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Paul Runge raped and killed 6 women and a young girl in Chicago, Illinois. The Police had suspected him in the early investigation but lacked the evidence to make an arrest.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A two-year-old boy was rushed to the hospital suffering from brain seizures and breathing difficulties.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When the decomposed body of a teenage girl is discovered, police have no clues to her identity. To solve the case, investigators turn to bug larvae found on the body.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Residents of Noel, Missouri were stunned to learn that their bank had been robbed and the bank president was missing. His body was later found floating in a lake, securely bound to a chair with duct tape.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A beautiful young woman falls to her death from a cliff overlooking the ocean. Initially, investigators think it was a tragic accident. But after a forensic examination, police uncover a very different picture of what happened.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Eleven-month-old Chad Shelton was admitted to the hospital with unexplained bleeding and later died. Investigators and scientists frantically searched for a cause when others from the same house fell ill.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: After a day of fishing in a small, quiet village in Switzerland, a teenage boy did not return home as planned. The investigation revealed some important microscopic evidence in the water near where he was last seen.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1992, 22-year-old Dawn Bruce is brutally murdered in her Virginia apartment. Investigators notice a blood smear on a pillowcase that appears to have been made by one of the killer's fingers.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Shortly after daybreak in Vancouver, British Columbia, a fire was set in a dumpster. No one saw either the arsonist or the fire, and it burned for hours in the deserted parking lot.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1993, Marie Robards suffered the loss of her father due to a heart attack - but it was more sinister.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A union official is executed in his home not long after a strike by the union membership. Neither his wife nor anyone in the neighborhood heard any gunshots.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A go-go dancer told her family she needed to get away for a while and then she simply disappeared. Investigators were suspicious, because she took neither her car nor her clothes. 20 years later, her family received a call that changed everything.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: While investigating the murder of 9-year-old Jessica Knott, police use a garbage bag to connect a suspect.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In the mid-1980s, bodies of nude women were found on a remote hill of California. The police got a break when a known prostitute escaped an attacker and was able to identify him and his automobile.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A young couple decided to celebrate their first wedding anniversary with a camping trip to Mount Hood. During the trip, the husband was shot and killed. Later, his wife told two different versions of the events which led to his death.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Timothy Wilson Spencer was identified as the man who raped and strangled five young women in Virginia. With the use of DNA profiling, an innocent man is freed and the real killer is convicted.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A woman is found dead from a gunshot wound and police suspect her husband, however, forensic teams prove what really happened, and the truth is stranger than fiction.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: For six years, a serial killer prowled NYC. He wrote letters to police indicating he would kill twelve people.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When Firefighters found an entire family dead inside their home it looked like a murder-suicide, but there were several inconsistent clues in the rubble.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1971, John List left a note with the bodies of his family members in his mansion before he disappeared.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Dianna Green was brutally attacked in her own home and her unborn child was killed. After coming out of a coma and regaining her memory, she is able to identify her husband as the perpetrator.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: While at a real estate conference in St. Louis, a man finds his wife drowned in the bathtub in their hotel room.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When the wife of a well-known dentist is found dead, police struggle to identify a suspect. Some fibers and a study of the weather patterns on the night of the murder break the case open.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A 1995 fire razes the mansion of a Kansas doctor, killing two of her three children; disturbing accounts from an ex-husband lead investigators to believe the doctor set the blaze herself.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A woman who suffered from mental illness is found dead. Was it due to natural causes, suicide or murder?

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: California police had several suspects in the robbery and brutal murder of an elderly couple. Ten years later, a determined forensic scientist used material from a dentist's office to uncover ballistic evidence that had been overlooked.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When police in the Great Plains are called about a dead body, they do a background check on the victim.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a policeman's house goes up in flames, experts suspect arson. The officer whose home was torched arrests a low-level drug dealer, who confesses. But then the suspect vanishes.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Discovering that an assault suspect is left-handed is crucial to a police investigation dating back 16 years.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1994, a human skull retrieved from an Ohio pond reveals a ghastly crime. Markings on the skull indicate that the victim had been stabbed multiple times and that the teeth had been removed with needle-nose pliers.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The wife of a murdered horse rancher explains that the last time she saw her husband was when he was leaving the house to go jogging. Police become suspicious when forensic evidence eventually leads investigators back to the ranch.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Authorities track a kidnapper who let his victim write a last will and informed the family of her murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Scott Dunn was missing and when the police sprayed his bedroom with Luminol, a scene of horrific violence emerged. Now investigators faced a daunting task.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: After more than 20 people on a Native American reservation die suddenly from a flu-like disease, Navajo medicine men provide a clue that leads to a most unlikely killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Series premiere. A flight attendant went missing and was never seen again. Police proved her husband guilty of murder without finding the body.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Almost a hundred vehicles were involved in the single most deadly automobile accident in American history.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: On the night of May 22, 1992, Betty Wilson returned home and walked up the stairs to the bedroom. She discovered her husband lying in a pool of blood. Jack Wilson had obviously been murdered...but how? And by whom? Even the experts couldn't agree.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Forensic computer analysis, blood spatter analysis and a painstaking investigation lead police to a lawyer husband Michael "Mick" Fletcher with a motive to kill his wife since he was having a sexual affair with judge Susan Chrzanowski.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.sub-title: Follow the Bike with No Light
programme.desc: A bicycle stopped for not having lights leads to the discovery of a stolen 27-foot trailer and its parolee occupants. Police respond to a brawl in a motel parking lot.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: Follow a SWAT mission from briefing to execution. A man wanted for assault with a deadly weapon is taken into custody.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A driver runs off after being involved in an accident. Deputies patrol a motel known for high drug activity.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.sub-title: Bikes for Sale: Cash, Money Order or Hash Oil Accepted
programme.desc: The West El Dorado Narcotics Enforcement Team conceive, plan and execute a ploy to catch a person suspected of offering hash oil to trade for goods on Craigslist.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A deputy responds to a house fire several days before Christmas. Later, he plays Santa to some kids who lost everything in the fire.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.sub-title: Grandma, Tell Me It Ain't So
programme.desc: An elderly woman claims she was assaulted, chocked and tied up by three armed assailants. A report of a woman acting erratically in the roadway. A juvenile claims his dad is beating up his mom.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A woman says her husband was stabbed by three teenagers. A deputy looks for trespassers at a location where drug users are known to congregate.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: Officers do a probation search of a known heroin user. A person with two felony warrants has a propensity to evade capture. A motorcyclist is pinned between vehicles after an accident.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: An inebriated man makes death threats after being removed from a bar. A deputy investigates a possible stolen vehicle in a grocery store parking lot. A man gives officers an unbelievable story.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: We follow Deputy Bryan Payne up close and personal on his night shift as he goes about a typical day for a deputy in El Dorado County.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: Detectives and deputies arrest sex traffickers in a prostitution sting operation. A store owner reports a suspicious man looking into car windows. A deputy is called to a business where four people sitting in a car appear to be up to no good.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: Deputies respond to a report of an inebriated man trying to change a tire on the side of the road. Police investigate a report of a suspicious person in the back of a store loading items onto his truck.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: Deputies are flagged down over an alleged case of domestic violence. A man armed with a shotgun claims that people on his property are out to hurt him.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.sub-title: A Big Dog & Bunches of Meth
programme.desc: Police respond to a scene where a person at gunpoint is in a vehicle reported as stolen. A man reportedly jumps from a moving car. Drugs are found in a vehicle that is stuck in a puddle.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: Police respond to a report of a man harassing people in a parking lot. A property agent is concerned about his tenant. A truck with an expired registration is pulled over.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A dangerous fugitive is wanted for assault and battery. A deputy stops an unregistered vehicle. A look at the daily procedures deputies must follow before going on patrol.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: An anonymous caller gives the whereabouts of a stolen vehicle. The driver of a car stopped at an intersection is unresponsive. A deputy responds to a report of a vehicle that may have been used in an earlier crime.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: Officers try to clear out several hostile transients who are camping illegally. A deputy describes a mental game he uses to keep him sharp while on patrol. A man refuses to leave a car dealership.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: An undercover narcotics team goes after a drug house. A drunk motorist drives over a cliff and into a river.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.sub-title: Nine-Time Loser Goes for His Tenth!
programme.desc: Police take down a huge marijuana grow operation run by a suspect who is a nine-time felon. A report of gunfire in a neighborhood. A man accuses his girlfriend of hitting him.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A deputy responds to a 9-1-1 call about a possible burglary. Deputies answer a domestic violence call, where a man claims his wife choked him.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: An anonymous caller gives the whereabouts of a stolen vehicle. The driver of a car stopped at an intersection is unresponsive. A deputy responds to a report of a vehicle that may have been used in an earlier crime.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A woman says her husband slammed her head into a car. A juvenile reports domestic violence between his parents.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: Police attempt to arrest a drug dealer. A young woman is found lying on the side of the road. Officers try to catch people who are willing to buy alcohol for minors.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.sub-title: A Day at the Drug House
programme.desc: A deputy follows two probationers into a known drug house. A water company worker contacting people about a water issue calls police about an extremely inebriated woman who fell down.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: The investigation into a case of domestic abuse leads to the arrest of the abuser's friend on a $25,000 warrant. A deputy investigates two women who are parked behind a closed business late at night.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A probationer has a warrant out for his arrest. Two people are found sleeping in a car behind a drug store.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: Two children are found riding on the top of a vehicle. A probation search on a lady who has outstanding warrants.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: Police seek to take down a known drug house where they expect many arrests. Deputies investigate a van parked after hours in a school parking area. Knives, bullets and marijuana are found in a suspicious car.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: An intoxicated young man exposes the dysfunctional mind of an alcoholic. A known drug user is found roaming a department store parking lot.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A deputy goes after suspicious subjects in a parking lot. An intoxicated person has an emergency protective order keeping him from seeing his wife. A woman claims her boyfriend took her baby away from her and left.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.sub-title: You Can Run But You Can Not Hide
programme.desc: Deputies search for a man wanted for felonious assault and domestic violence. Police make a traffic stop on a person they had arrested in the past.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.sub-title: Smile, You're on My Surveillance System!
programme.desc: The Special Enforcement Detail prepares to take down a person with a felony warrant for theft and drug possession. Deputies investigating a disturbance in a field discover minors in possession of drugs and alcohol.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A troubled young man with physical limitations decides to fight with the police. The boat patrol on Lake Tahoe responds to an overturned vessel in the water. A vehicle with an out-of-date registration is pulled over.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: Deputies are flagged down over an alleged case of domestic violence. A man armed with a shotgun claims that people on his property are out to hurt him.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: Police respond to a report of a stabbing at a housing complex.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A deputy responds to a 9-1-1 call reporting a verbal argument between a man and a woman.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.sub-title: Pot, Pipe Bombs and Clueless People
programme.desc: Police execute a search warrant on a marijuana grow operation run by illegal Mexican nationals. A person reports they found three pipe bombs. In a domestic violence call, a man is reported to be burning all his own property on his front lawn.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: Deputies search a house for a domestic violence offender and his wife is pretending not to know his location; a probation search on a repeat offender.

programme.title: Sheriffs: El Dorado County
programme.desc: A deputy checks in on a woman who is on probation and is about to be evicted from the motel room she's living in. A man's neighbors claim he is driving drunk. A traffic stop leads to a drug charge.

programme.desc: Barnaby and Winter discover a dead body covered in live rabbits.
